key_insight: An accelerated PF-CZM framework can simulate mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue of UHPC and HSS efficiently by combining a stress-based failure criterion with fatigue degradation and load-envelope acceleration.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical computational tool for assessing durability of cyclic-loaded infrastructure materials when full experimental fatigue campaigns are too costly or slow.
method: Derive a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests to compute crack driving force.
Embed a fatigue degradation function into the PF-CZM fracture model.
Use an acceleration algorithm with envelope loading and adaptive cyclic increments to approximate high-cycle fatigue without simulating every cycle.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and is reported to reproduce mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior for both UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: The acceleration scheme reduces computational cost but may require careful calibration to ensure the envelope-load approximation remains accurate across different loading histories and material states.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
